evals.prompt: A technical founder describes their product's copy vibe: 'direct but warm — we're the guide, not the expert. Our audience is technical founders and their teams.' Name the copy direction.
evals.expected_output: A small set of NAMED copy goals, each a noun phrase a non-designer can recall, RANKED so a tie can break, with a single dominant goal that wins when goals conflict. Each goal grounded in at least one stable referent (persona language, a named copy precedent, or a persuasion standard). At least one arbitration rule recorded (which goal wins which conflict and why). No finished copy strings or formula tables. VoC posture declared (directional if elicited inline).
assertions: Names the primary reader type and writes or references a copy JTBD sentence
Produces at least two named copy goals, each a short noun phrase (not a sentence or a single vague word)
Ranks the goals and names a single dominant goal that breaks ties
Each goal has at least one stable referent: persona language, a named copy precedent, or a named persuasion standard
Records at least one arbitration rule (conflict type → winner → reason)
Does NOT produce finished copy strings, formula tables, or pre-written headlines
If VoC is absent, flags goals as 'directional — not backed by VoC research'
Artifact path docs/design/copy/<slug>.md stated or implied
